attributes.description: Alerts when peak (Maximum) Lambda invocation duration exceeds a configurable threshold, indicating tail latency or cold-start regressions.
attributes.name: [AWS Lambda OTel] High tail duration

investigation_guide.blob:
## High Tail Duration

### Triage Steps
1. Tail latency uses CloudWatch `Maximum` statistic — true percentiles are not available from this source.
2. Check for cold starts: correlate with low `ConcurrentExecutions` or recent scale-from-zero events.
3. Review VPC-attached functions for ENI initialization delays.
4. Compare tail vs average duration — a large gap often points to cold starts or rare slow paths.
5. Consider provisioned concurrency if cold starts breach user-facing SLOs.

### Note
Adjust the threshold to match your tail-latency SLO. The default targets severe outliers, not typical p99 behaviour.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.lambda.otel-*
| WHERE attributes.Namespace == "AWS/Lambda"
    AND attributes.MetricName == "Duration"
    AND attributes.stat == "Maximum"
| STATS max_duration_ms = MAX(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/Lambda/Duration`)
  BY attributes.FunctionName, resource.attributes.cloud.region
// Tail latency threshold (ms): catches cold starts and severe outliers
| WHERE max_duration_ms > 10000
| EVAL function_id = CONCAT(attributes.FunctionName, "/", resource.attributes.cloud.region)
| SORT max_duration_ms DESC
| LIMIT 10
